title: Hospital Management Module – Phase 1: Frontend Implementation

requirements.summary: Add Hospital Management page under Super Admin navigation with list, search, pagination, and action buttons
acceptanceCriteria: HospitalManagementPage is accessible only to superAdmin role (guarded by RoleGuard).
Hospitals list is paginated at 20 per page with next/previous controls.
Search input filters visible results by name, city, or area in real time.
Each row shows an Active/Inactive status badge.
Empty state message 'No hospitals added yet.' is shown when no hospitals exist.
Disable button triggers a confirmation dialog before calling disableHospital.

file_operations.description: Create a new HospitalManagementPage component listing hospitals with search bar, pagination (20 per page), Active/Inactive status badges, Edit and Disable action buttons. Use the useHospitals hook for fetching paginated data with search filter. Show 'No hospitals added yet.' empty state when no data exists. Wrap Edit and Disable buttons with role checks for superAdmin. Include a confirmation dialog for disabling hospitals.

Add a lazy-loaded route for '/staff-app/hospital-management' that renders HospitalManagementPage. Import the page component dynamically using React.lazy. Register the route in the onNavigate switch statement under the superAdmin role condition.

Add 'Hospital Management' navigation item to the superAdmin role configuration with path '/staff-app/hospital-management' and icon 'Hospital' (Lucide icon name). Insert it in the appropriate position within the superAdmin nav items array.

requirements.summary: Create Add Hospital modal with form fields and validation, calling addHospital mutation
acceptanceCriteria: Hospital Name field shows a validation error if submitted empty.
Form calls addHospital mutation on submit and shows a success toast on completion.
Modal closes and list refreshes after successful creation.

file_operations.description: Create a modal dialog component with a form containing fields: Hospital Name (required text input with validation), City (text input), Address (textarea), Area/Zone (text input), Contact Number (text input), and Active/Inactive toggle (switch/checkbox defaulting to active). Use react-hook-form for validation. On submit, call the useAddHospital mutation hook. Show success toast on completion and close modal. Handle errors with toast notifications.

Add an 'Add Hospital' button at the top of HospitalManagementPage that opens the AddHospitalModal. Import and render AddHospitalModal with open/close state management. After successful hospital creation, invalidate the hospitals query to refresh the list.

requirements.summary: Create Edit Hospital modal pre-populated with existing data, calling updateHospital mutation
acceptanceCriteria: All existing field values are pre-filled when the edit modal opens.
Saving calls updateHospital and shows a success toast.
Only superAdmin can see the Edit button.

file_operations.description: Create a modal dialog component that accepts a Hospital object as a prop and pre-fills a form with fields: Hospital Name (required), City, Address, Area/Zone, Contact Number, and Active/Inactive toggle. Use react-hook-form with default values from the passed hospital. On submit, call the useUpdateHospital mutation with the hospital ID and updated fields. Show success toast on completion and close modal. Handle errors with toast notifications.

Add Edit buttons in each hospital row that open EditHospitalModal with the selected hospital data. Import and render EditHospitalModal with open/close state and selected hospital state management. After successful update, invalidate the hospitals query to refresh the list.

requirements.summary: Create Hospital Details page with Assigned Phlebotomists section and assignment controls
acceptanceCriteria: Assigned phlebotomists list is shown with name and area.
Assign Phlebotomist button is visible only to superAdmin.
Multi-select dropdown supports text search to filter phlebotomists.
Area-match suggestion label appears next to phlebotomists sharing the hospital's area.
Saving assignment calls assignPhlebotomistToHospital and refreshes the list.
If no phlebotomists exist, show an appropriate empty state.

file_operations.description: Create HospitalDetailsPage component showing hospital details at the top and an 'Assigned Phlebotomists' section below. Use useHospitalById to fetch hospital data and useGetPhlebotomistsByHospital to fetch assigned phlebotomists with their area information. Display each assigned phlebotomist with name and area. For superAdmin role, show an 'Assign Phlebotomist' button that opens a multi-select dropdown. The dropdown should list all phlebotomists (fetched via a query), support text search/filtering, and show '(Same area as hospital)' suggestion text next to phlebotomists whose area matches the hospital's area. On saving selections, call useAssignPhlebotomistToHospital for each newly selected phlebotomist. Show empty state 'No phlebotomists available' if none exist. Include a remove/unassign button per phlebotomist that calls useRemovePhlebotomistFromHospital with a removal reason.

Add a lazy-loaded route for '/staff-app/hospital-management/:hospitalId' that renders HospitalDetailsPage. Import the page component dynamically using React.lazy. Register the route in the onNavigate switch statement under the superAdmin role condition.
Make each hospital row clickable or add a 'View Details' button/link that navigates to '/staff-app/hospital-management/{hospitalId}' to open HospitalDetailsPage.

requirements.summary: Add Assigned Hospitals section to Phlebotomist Profile page with role-based controls
acceptanceCriteria: Phlebotomist profile page shows an 'Assigned Hospitals' section.
SuperAdmin sees add and remove controls for each assigned hospital entry.
Phlebotomist and Lab Admin roles see the section as read-only.
Removing a hospital calls removePhlebotomistFromHospital and updates the list.

file_operations.description: Add an 'Assigned Hospitals' section to ProfilePage that displays hospitals assigned to the current user (if phlebotomist role). Use useGetHospitalsByPhlebotomist hook to fetch assigned hospital IDs and useHospitalById for each to get full details (name, city, area). For superAdmin viewing a phlebotomist profile, show add/remove controls. For phlebotomist and labAdmin roles, display the list as read-only. When removing a hospital (superAdmin only), call useRemovePhlebotomistFromHospital with a removal reason and refresh the assigned hospitals list. Show empty state 'No hospitals assigned' if list is empty.

requirements.summary: Filter hospital selection on AddHospitalSamplePage to show only active hospitals assigned to the logged-in phlebotomist
acceptanceCriteria: Hospital dropdown on AddHospitalSamplePage only shows active hospitals assigned to the current phlebotomist.
If no hospitals are assigned, show a clear empty state message.

file_operations.description: Update the hospital selection logic to filter hospitals using both useGetHospitalsByPhlebotomist (to get assigned hospital IDs) and useHospitals (to get hospital details). Only display hospitals where isActive is true AND the hospital ID is in the assigned list. If the filtered list is empty, show an empty state message such as 'No active hospitals assigned to you. Please contact your administrator.' Ensure the form prevents submission if an invalid or inactive hospital is somehow selected, displaying a descriptive error message.

requirements.summary: Add React Query hooks for all new hospital management endpoints
acceptanceCriteria: All mutation hooks invalidate the relevant hospital and assignment query caches on success.
useHospitals accepts page and searchTerm parameters.
Hooks follow the existing pattern in useQueries.ts.

file_operations.description: Add React Query hooks for hospital management: useHospitals (query accepting searchTerm parameter for filtering by name/city/area), useHospitalById (query accepting hospitalId), useAddHospital (mutation for creating hospitals), useUpdateHospital (mutation for updating hospitals), useDisableHospital (mutation for soft-disabling hospitals), useAssignPhlebotomistToHospital (mutation for assigning phlebotomists), useRemovePhlebotomistFromHospital (mutation for removing assignments with removalReason), useGetPhlebotomistsByHospital (query accepting hospitalId and returning assigned phlebotomist principals with area info), and useGetHospitalsByPhlebotomist (query accepting phlebotomist principal and returning assigned hospital IDs). Ensure all mutation hooks invalidate the relevant query keys on success (e.g., useAddHospital invalidates ['hospitals'], useAssignPhlebotomistToHospital invalidates both ['phlebotomistsByHospital', hospitalId] and ['hospitalsByPhlebotomist', phlebotomist]). Follow existing patterns from useQueries.ts for error handling and cache invalidation.
